subtitle: A Compliance Burden Report

framing.thesis: The complaint crisis has three faces: volume explosion, scam-driven disputes, and furnisher liability. All three land on compliance teams.
framing.audience: Compliance, risk, and ops leaders at banks, credit unions, fintechs, BaaS platforms
framing.tone: Tension, not stats. Headlines that make compliance officers forward to their boss.

act_1.title: The Flood
act_1.subtitle: Volume Story
act_1.headline: Complaints doubled. Headcount didn't.
key_stats: AI agents and credit repair orgs flooding system

act_2.title: The Denials
act_2.subtitle: Scam Liability Story
act_2.headline: 94% of money transfer claims denied. Regulators are watching.
key_stats: Banks citing 'authorized' via PIN, text confirmation
CFPB sued Zelle, fined Cash App $175M

act_3.title: The Corrections
act_3.subtitle: Furnisher Liability Story
act_3.headline: 40% of credit reporting complaints require corrections. That's not consumer error. That's your data.
key_stats: Consumers denied jobs and apartments due to errors
77% of ALL complaints are about Equifax, Experian, or TransUnion

data_files.description: Total complaints, year-over-year trends, product breakdown
How companies responded - monetary, non-monetary, explanation, admin
Credit reporting complaints - 88% of all complaints
Money transfer complaints - scam liability story
Checking/savings complaints - fraud claims and denials
AI agents, credit repair orgs, complaint flooding
CFPB enforcement actions, Reg E pressure, legislative signals

sections_cut.reason: SAVE litigation noise, not bank compliance

headline_options: 94% of money transfer claims denied. Regulators are watching.
40% of credit reporting complaints require corrections. That's not consumer error. That's your data.
6.6 million complaints. One CRA flagged 470,000 as suspected fraud.
The 'authorized' defense is running out of time.
